entry.title: Applying an SLT filtering to an existing replication
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team ,

We would like to introduce a filter condition on one of the Fields (e.g., excluding records where the Field value is not equal to <xxxx>).

Could you please confirm whether this can be achieved by simply suspending the replication, applying the filter, and then resuming it?

Alternatively, would it be necessary to completely stop the replication and restart it with the new filter condition?

Also, are there any recommended approaches to implement this change without disrupting or stopping the ongoing replication?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Applying an SLT filtering to an existing replication
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

You can apply a filter without reloading a table, per KBA 2581611 - How to configure filters/rules for replication - SLT - "If adding a filter in Filter Options or Rule assignment while a table is in replication regenerating the migration objects is required - 2776058 - How to regenerate the migration object for a table? - SLT"

One thing to be aware of is that SLT will not automatically drop the data from the target that doesn't meet the filter requirement, the only way to have SLT drop that data is by reloading the table with the Refresh Behavior Truncate or Drop - 2549915 - How to avoid dropping data or tables when reloading a table - SLT

 

Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Fundamentals
entry.content: Could you share a bit about your background;  whether you are more interested in the functional / business process side of SAP or the technical / development side? Also, do you have prior professional experience in IT, finance, or business operations? This will help me suggest the most relevant fundamental SAP learning materials for you.

entry.title: Performance and Goals - Story Reports
entry.content:
Hello,
I’m having some difficulties getting started with a query to create story reports for the Performance and Goals module.

The goal is to track, for each employee, which step they are currently in, as well as the changes made by each form participant (employee, manager, manager’s manager, etc.).

Does anyone have any suggestions?

I would really appreciate any help you can provide.
Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors 2H 2025: Seamless Talent Data Transfer from Inactive to Active Employment
entry.content:
Hi @SAP_Ragini 

I have a specific question on this topic.

Recruiters make primary Employment inactive and from Secondary employment the employee cannot apply for the internal job from the career site

This is happening mainly because, once Primary Employment terminated the Internal Candidate profile is converted to External Candidate profile and hence the employee could not apply for the new job even though the secondary employments are still active

Can this feature help if i transfer the talent data from Inactive (Primary Employment) to Active (Secondary employment)

Additionally, though all the prerequisites are met, i still can't access the Manage Individual Talent Data Transfer tool

entry.title: Re: How to enable Print Forms Service after 2025 update?
entry.content:
Hi @dariavalgutskova ,

I hope to find you well!

After the migration of PFS, there is no need for the user, password, or URL. Also, it is not necessary to enable it. 

The migration makes sure systems are provisioned with PFS, without any need for configuration.

Best regards,
Jacson
SAP Support

entry.title: Training material for situation handling as we intend to implement for the Customer on Public Cloud

Could you please provide some training material for situation handling as we intend to implement for a customer from the scratch?

Regards

Ashish

entry.title: Modeling Guide for Post Processing Framework (PPF)
entry.content:
I started working on the technical side of SAP EWM from last year and finding it quite interesting.

I came across How-To Guides for SAP Extended Warehouse Management and from there I got the PDF for the How to use PPF in EWM from this link PPF_in_SAP_EWM_2021.pdf.

But this PDF talks about 2 prerequisites 
You can find additional information in the following places:
• SAP Library: Post Processing Framework (BC-SRV-GBT) (PPF)
• SAP Community Network: Modeling Guide for Post Processing Framework (PPF) (Article)

Both of the links go to 404 error.
I am wondering if I can get these latest documents from somewhere.

I searched on SDN and SAP support portal but could not find.
I got this SDN post PPF Actions: Recursive action call saving the docu... - SAP Community but the link there is also not accessible.

Could someone point out with the link to get all the technical information of SAP EWM?

Kind regards,
Himanshu Gupta

entry.title: Re: Setup Event Mesh – Integration Suite (EMIS) to Trigger Events from S4H Cloud to CPI via Webhooks
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
How many connections can you have with an EMIS instance?

I'm currently using 250 with Event Mesh Standalone.

I'm trying to change the settings, but I can't get past 50 connections:

{
“resources”: {
“units”: 50
},
“rules”: {
“topicRules”: {
“publishFilter”: [
“${namespace}/*”
],
“subscribeFilter”: [
“*”
]
},
“queueRules”: {
“publishFilter”: [
“${namespace}/*”
],
“subscribeFilter”: [
“${namespace}/*”
]
}
},
“name”: “emis”,
“namespace”: “xxx/xx/1”,
“serviceInstanceId”: “xxxx”
}

entry.title: Is This Really How Automatic Valuation Should Work in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In a recent Quality Management implementation in #SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, I came across something that struck me as unexpected — and I am hoping the SAP Community can confirm whether this is intended system behavior, a limitation, or if I’m missing a detail in configuration.
 
We have an inspection setup with:
  • An inspection plan containing two quantitative characteristics
  • Each characteristic has tolerance limits defined
  • Multiple samples are recorded
  • All sample results are within tolerance
  • Each sample is correctly auto‑valuated (Accepted/Rejected)
So far, everything works as expected.
But then comes the issue.

Even though all sample valuations are green, the Master Inspection Characteristic (MIC) itself is:
  • Not automatically valuated
  • Not set to Accepted
  • Still requires manual valuation before I am allowed to make the Usage Decision
Is it really intended that a quantitative MIC with tolerance limits still requires manual valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — even when all sample results are within specification? From a customer perspective, this is a tedious extra step. 
 
PLM Quality Management (QM) EWM - Quality Management

entry.title: Re: Reimagining Certification for the Way We Work Today
entry.content:
Hello!

@omar-sharaf82, thank you for comments.

I still have others questions:

 

1. Where could I try to pass mock questions for the scenario-based exam C_ACT? There were a lot of multi-choice mock tests and exams, for example on Udemy. But I can't find a scenario-based exam anywhere.

 

3. What subscribers really need for taking the scenario-based exam C_ACT - 2/6 exam attempts or full SAP learning hub subscription? When I try to register for the exam, the system asks me to subscribe to SAP Learning Hub.

 

4. Is there useful material for the scenario-based exam C_ACT on SAP Learning Hub? Or can I finish my preparation for SAP without a Learning Hub subscription?

 

Thank you for attention!

entry.title: Re: Cant we configure delete stock identification , remov...
entry.content:
Hi Anjanna,

Delete Stock Identification flag on Storage Type customizing (STOCKID_DEL) can be set for both, final and non-final Storage Types as mentioned on F1 (help):

"Delete Stock Identification

Controls whether the stock identification of stock in this storage type is to be deleted.

Use

If you have set the Stor.Ctrl/Put.Compl. indicator, that is, you are working with final storage types, you also have to select this checkbox for these final storage types.

You can also select this checkbox for other storage types. This makes the system delete the stock identification before putaway from the respective stock has been completed."

Regards,

João

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Wroclaw - April edition!
entry.content:
You read that right! We have another SAP Stammtisch in Wroclaw! 

Let's have fun, order nice food and talk about everything SAP-related! 

  1. Fun and opportunity to meet cool SAPers is obviously free:)
  2. Food - everyone pay for themselves
  3. (First) drinks - sponsored by Capgemini! 

Where? Drevny Kocur OVO Wroclaw
When? 17.04.2026, 18.00-21.00
How many people can participate? There are only 25 places!

Simple rules of signing up:

On the left you have question Will you be attending? Click Yes - it's that easy!

You clicked "Yes", but can't come? It's even simpler - click Not Going

entry.title: Re: What Is Agentic AI and Why SAP Teams Are Paying Attention in 2026
entry.content: Very insightful overview of agentic AI in SAP landscapes. As a SAP Signavio and BTM process transformation consultant, I clearly see how well‑governed process models and KPIs in Signavio can provide the ‘structured backbone’ these agents need to monitor deviations and trigger actions safely. I’m especially interested in concrete patterns for combining deterministic process/decision rules with LLM‑based agents in scenarios lije these.

entry.title: Re: My Time Event (F1506B) Frontend Extension
entry.content:
Dear Vibha,

Hello and greetings.
The team can try to leverage the app's details to their manual.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c6c3ffd90792427a9fee1a19df5b0925/9911873518ff41d0ab21d370963a464d.html 

in that link you have extension points.

It also may be tied to the UI controller hooks.

ControllerHook NamePurpose
S1.controller.jsextHookChangeFooterButtonsAdd, remove, or modify buttons in the footer of the Create view.
S1.controller.jsextHookCreatePostObjectModify the data object right before it is sent to the backend for creation.
S1.controller.jsextHookOnCreateInject custom logic that triggers immediately when the user clicks 'Create'.
S1.controller.jsextHookOnDeleteAdd custom validation or logging when a time event is deleted.
S1.controller.jsextHookOnChangeOfDateExecute logic when the user selects a different date in the calendar.
S2.controller.jsextHookOnUpdateSimilar to the create hook, but specifically for the Update/Edit view.


or XML fragments.

or Backend Badls (Business Add-Ons).

For now, that is what I can share.

Regards,
Mark

entry.title: Re: Multiple Purchase Organization for one plant
entry.content:
Hello @JFleischhauer,

unlike @Lakshmipathi i believe that you can indeed create two separate purchasing organizations.

My opinion is based on the following Help Portal entry Organizational Units | SAP Help Portal where the relation from Plant to Purchasing Organization is described as n:n.

This also aligns with info that can be found in the Learning Journey Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement > Unit 2: Identifying the Organizational Structure in Procurement.

 

A quick guide on how to add an additional Purchasing Organization (see Changes via Edit | SAP Help Portal / Setting Up Organizational Structure | SAP Help Portal)  :

1) Open the Set Up Organizational Structure activity. Choose the Edit button.                                                                               

  • Choose whether you want to manually change the status of Configure Your Business Processes back to Open.                         
  • The status of the already confirmed activities will be set to Reopened.

2) The organization structure is editable now. Here you need to add the additional Purchasing Organization.

3) Complete editing by choosing Complete Activity.

Afterwards, the new organizational struture must be deployed (see: Deploying Scope and Organizational Structure | SAP Help Portal).

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Receiving History
entry.content: Receipts of non-stock materials won't be visible there, unless 101 receipts are actually posted.

entry.title: Re: Give Your AI Agent Some Tools: Introducing the UI5 MCP Server
entry.content: @guillaume-hrc This is a really interesting topic. We monitor the current developments in offering better In-App support for LLMs. One approach which sounds promising is https://github.com/webmachinelearning/webmcp There is already a pure JS library implementing the basic functionality, so you might use it in your project.

entry.title: Re: Stock Deficit Report for Delivery Document Items with...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there is no single “deficit stock” report that consolidates all shortages shown in error messages like “Deficit of SL Unrestrict-use 1 PC”. Instead, you need to use standard Fiori apps such as Stock – Multiple Materials, Monitor Material Coverage, or Stock Overview to identify materials with insufficient stock. These apps allow you to filter for negative or deficit quantities across plants and storage locations

entry.title: ECP contract generation from IT0016 + SuccessFactors DocuSign integration
entry.content:
Hi all,

Has anyone implemented a process where the labor contract is generated in ECP (based on IT0016) and then sent for signature through DocuSign using the standard SAP SuccessFactors – DocuSign integration?

The idea would be for SuccessFactors to send the document directly for signature, without using Integration Suite / middleware between IT0016 and DocuSign.

In other words, we are wondering if this could be handled more or less as a standard SuccessFactors document template with the "signature" option enabled = true, even if the contract-related data originates from ECP / IT0016.

Or, from your experience, would it be more recommended to generate the labor contract directly in SuccessFactors instead?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content: week 4 completed : package - ZAC_554860

entry.title: Re: Receiving History
entry.content:
Hello @fcancellieri 

You want to see receipts of non-stock materials, so you need to post those receipts. Yes, receipts of non-stock materials can be posted, the same way as regular receipts of stock materials, with 101 movement type, in MIGO for instance. The only difference is that a non-stock material receipt is debited to a cost account, whereas regular receipt are debited to stock accounts.

Whether receipts of non-stock materials are allowed or if they are obligatory is dependent on account assignment configuration (SPRO: Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Account Assignment -> Maintain Account Assignment Categories):

That is how it works in S/4HANA on-premise / private cloud. Hopefully, it is the same in the public cloud.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: How ot enable new/additional team categories as recepiant in situation handeling in public cloud?
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Year end Activities of MM

entry.title: Unable to set property from an XML using the XPath.
entry.content:
Hello Experts.

I am unable to set a property using an XPATH from the XML Payload.

Following is the XML payload.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<entry xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices"
xml:base="https://api41preview.sapsf.com/odata/v2/">
<content type="application/xml">hello</content>
<d:fileExtension>txt</d:fileExtension>
</entry>

Note that I have simplified this payload eliminating all the other nodes so as to focus on the question at hand. 

Following is the sample I-flow I created to explain the issue.

In Content Modifier 1 I have hardcoded the body with the Payload

In Content Modifier 2 I am setting the Property using the Xpath as follows

Action Name Type Data Type Value

ActionNameTypeData TypeValue

Create

fileExtension

XPath

java.lang.String

/entry/d:fileExtension/text()

In Content Modifier 3 I am checking if the Property is set. This is where I find that the property is not getting set.

I have confirmed that the XPATH is correct testing it wit other tools.

I have also included the namespaces d and m on the Runtime Configuration Tab on the Integration Flow as follows

entry.title: Second handed VAT process in SAP Public Cloud Portugal
entry.content:
Hello all,

We are implementing SAP Public Cloud. Our customer buys and sells second handed goods. How can customize it in the system? Is there any standard process that covers this?

Thanks,

Marlene

entry.title: SNOTE deimplement error SCWN622 You cannot delete objects of type & (DDIC)
entry.content:
I'm deimplementing the note 3415841 - Asynchronous export to spreadsheet from generic Web Dynpro analytic grid app - SAP for Me and get the error SCWN 622 "You cannot delete objects of type &":

  • DOMD FPM_BICS_STATUS
  • DTED FPM_BICS_STATUS
  • TABD FPM_BICS_ASYNC
  • TABT FPM_BICS_ASYNC

Screenshot in French (text of message SCWN 622: "Les suppressions des objets de type & ne sont pas supportées.")

How to fix it?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: SNOTE deimplement error SCWN622 You cannot delete objects of type & (DDIC)
entry.content:
The solution is explained by this note:

3332588 - Error "You cannot delete objects of type Table" during de-implementing note - SAP for Me.

It explains to continue the deimplementation and delete the DDIC objects manually as soon as possible. Don't forget to "write down" the list of objects to delete because they are not shown in the log. In the case of the note 3415841, run SE11 to delete the table FPM_BICS_ASYNC, then delete the data element FPM_BICS_STATUS and the domain FPM_BICS_STATUS.

There's also the note 2840923 - SNOTE: Enable De-implementation of DDIC Correction Instructions - SAP for Me, it permits only to de-implement modifications, not creations, i.e. for de-implementing DDIC object creations, you need to delete these DDIC objects manually as explained in the note 3332588.

entry.title: Re: Unable to set property from an XML using the XPath.
entry.content:
The problem is on account of the fact that the root element has an unnamed namespace.  If you use the following XPath expression you will observe that the property is set - /*:entry/d:fileExtension/text()

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 9: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile
entry.content:
Hi @mkarthikeyas 

Have a look at this news article from our chief AI officer Jonathon von Rueden: https://news.sap.com/2026/03/why-is-generative-ui-the-new-frontier-for-business-software/. Most likely you'll find out more in May at Sapphire 2026, so sign-up either in-person or for the virtual event.

Regards,

   Tom

entry.title: Re: Training material for situation handling as we intend...
entry.content:
Hi Ashish,

Please find all the document about this.

Situation Handling – Standard Framework. (Including the Responsibility Management)

  • SAP Help Portal
    • Situation Handling
      • Predelivered use cases
      • Standard framework of Situation Handling
    • Responsibility Management
  • SAP Best Practices
    • Situation Handling (31N)
    • Responsibility Management (1NJ)
  • SAP Community
    • Intelligent Situation Handling
  • Blog Posts
  • Introduction series – standard framework
    • Situation Handling: What is it and why do you need it? (1/5)
    • Help! Why Don’t I Get a Situation Notification?
  • Responsibility Management
    • Understand how responsibility teams and rules work together with Situation Handling

 

Situation Handling – Extended Framework. (Including the Responsibility Management)

You find several blog posts that help you starting with custom situations in SAP Community Intelligent Situation Handling:

  • Concept of custom situations
    • The Power of Reuse – Custom Situation Configuration
  • Design your situation case
    • How to Start with Custom Situations: High-Level Design of the Use Case (1/4)
  • Configure your situation case
    • Custom Situation Cases: Configure Your Own Use Cases (1/6)
  • Custom layout & custom data in My Situations – Extended app
    • Fine-Tuning of Custom Situations: Configure the Layout of My Situations – Extended

SAP Help Portal

  • Situation Handling
    • Predelivered use cases
    • Extended framework of Situation Handling
  • Responsibility Management

SAP Best Practices

  • Situation Handling (31N)
  • Responsibility Management (1NJ)

SAP Community

  • Intelligent Situation Handling

Blog Posts

Introduction series – extended framework

  • Custom Situation Cases: Configure Your Own Use Cases (1/6)
  • Help! Why don’t I get a notification for my custom situation?

Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • How Developer Extensibility lets you create your own Situation Handling Use Case (1/3)

Responsibility Management

  • Understand how responsibility teams and rules work together with Situation Handling

entry.title: Re: SAP code Jam request for Eindhoven, Netherlands 2026
entry.content: I've recorded your request.  Do you have any particular time frame in mind? Or specific times that would NOT work?

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP TS-Based Supply Finite Heuristic - TRATIO
entry.content:
Hi,

the finite heuristic does not support TRATIO. Instead it supports quota arrangement coverage for location source (lpquotacoverage).
But please consider the limitation listed here:
"Quota arrangements, although the finite heuristic always prioritizes demand fulfillment and so might ignore any quota arrangements to fulfill demand.

The finite heuristic also doesn't support certain aspects of quota arrangements, for example:

  • Sources of supply having different quotas in the same period.

  • Sources of supply prioritized based on costs when considering a quota; instead quotas take priority."

Best regards, Carsten

entry.title: Re: Data masking, data scrambling and data anonymization in Business Data Cloud with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello,

Great post! This framework is incredibly helpful for automating anonymization views.

I wanted to share a quick tip for anyone encountering a specific SQL syntax error during execution. If you see an error like incorrect syntax near ",": line 1 col 57 coming from the EXEC_VIEWS_CREATOR procedure, check your RENAME_TO column in the parameters table.

In the provided logic, if a column is set to CREATE = 'YES', the RENAME_TO field must be populated. If you don't actually want to rename the column, you should still enter the original column name in that field. If left blank (NULL), the dynamic SQL generator creates an empty space between the commas in the CREATE VIEW (...) and SELECT ... clauses, leading to a syntax failure.

Error example for reference:

sql syntax error: ... "EXEC_VIEWS_CREATOR": line 52 col 5: incorrect syntax near ","

Once I ensured every active row had a value in RENAME_TO, the task chain worked perfectly. Thanks again for the guide!

entry.title: Issue in Customer Clearing – Document Splitting Currency Overflow Error
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I would like to report an issue encountered during the customer clearing process in the “Clear Incoming Payments” app.

Error Message:
“Document splitting causes field overflow for currency type 10 in row 000”

Scenario:
We are trying to clear two DA documents against one DR document, but the system consistently throws the above error during posting.

Troubleshooting Done:

  • Attempted clearing one DA document with the DR document while keeping the remaining balance open
  • Cleared DA documents individually against the DR document
  • Selected all documents together and used the “Post on Account” option

However, the issue persists in all scenarios.

Additional Observation:

  • The issue occurs only when clearing DA with DR documents
  • The DA document was originally created through the same customer clearing process
  • Currently, DR document type is being used for clearing transactions

Could you please help us with:

  • Possible root cause of this issue and Resolution.

Your guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thanks & Regards,

Rohini Dutta

entry.title: Unable to login to SAP BTP Trial account
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am unable to login to SAP BTP Trial account. I am getting the below message.

Anyone facing this issue?

Thanks,

BK

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud TRM – Approval process for Treasury Payments
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are currently implementing Treasury & Risk Management (TRM) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and are looking for guidance on how best to introduce an approval workflow for Treasury payments only, without impacting supplier invoice payments (AP).

Current State:

  • We are on S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Treasury payments are executed via F111
  • An approval workflow is currently configured at the bank master level

Challenges:

Bank‑level approval applies to all outbound payments using that bank account. As a result, both TRM payments and supplier invoice payments go through the same approval process. Our goal is to narrow the approval scope to TRM transactions only.

Questions: 

  1. Can SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud support an approval workflow after TRM deal settlement but before payment execution (F111), without using approval at bank master level so it won't impacting AP payments?
  2. If payment-level approval is not supported, can approval be enforced before Fix / Post / Pay so that Treasury payments can only be created after Treasury Manager approval?
  3. If neither is possible, can approval be restricted to a dedicated Treasury payment method so that only TRM payments require approval and AP payments remain unaffected?

Any insights, real‑world experience, or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: How to send sap ABAP audit log data to Splunk?
entry.content:
I am looking for some documentation or any samples/examples on how to send HTTP requests to Splunk HEC from ABAP audit log

I am looking for best way to send an event from ABAP to Splunk?

entry.title: Governing Possible Future
entry.content:
In my previous post on 'Governing the Emerging Collective Intelligence', I explored an idea that has stayed with me: That AI is not just processing data -it is reorganising and externalising fragments of our collective knowledge, decisions, and experience. But what happens when that intelligence becomes operational inside a system like Sales Performance Management? Why did SPM always feel like the ideal space for AI to me? I’ve been asking myself this question for some time. SPM somehow brings everything together in a way that feels… almost as if it were designed for AI. There is a constant need for optimisation. There is a need for guided navigation, not just reporting. There is demand for real-time visibility. And there is always the question of prediction -what comes next, what will I earn, what should I focus on? It is not a static system. It is alive.

But I think the real reason is something deeper. SPM is one of the rare places where data does not stay abstract. It becomes personal. Every transaction, every rule, every adjustment - created by different people across the organisation - ultimately shapes a single outcome: compensation.

And that immediately raises questions: Why is my payout like this? Is this correct? What does it mean for me? What should I do next?

These are not just data questions. They are questions of interpretation. And now, increasingly, they are answered by AI. That’s why SPM feels like such a natural fit. Not because it “can use AI” - but because it already behaves like a system that is waiting for a cognitive layer.

At the same time, this realisation changes something. If AI starts explaining outcomes, guiding decisions, and influencing behaviour… then we are no longer just implementing AI in SPM. We are shaping how people understand their value, their performance, and their future.

Which makes me wonder: Are we building intelligent systems…or are we beginning to govern a form of collective intelligence?

If we look at it structurally, the core loop of such a system could be described as:

DATA → INTERPRETATION → DECISION → MOTIVATION → BEHAVIOUR → DATA

Mapped to SPM:

DATA->Transactions, credits, quotas, hierarchies

INTERPRETATION (AI enters here)->“Why did I earn this?” ->Assistants/agents

DECISION->Where to focus, what deals to prioritise

MOTIVATION->Incentives, fairness perception, trust

BEHAVIOUR->Sales actions, performance changes

→ feeding back into DATA

 And yet, something is still missing. A governing layer.

GOVERNANCE OF INTELLIGENCE /Rules & compensation logic/Data quality -the “system of truth”/AI explanations and boundaries/Fairness and transparency/Ownership — who defines the logic?

SPM is not just a system of record. It is behaving as a governed loop of collective intelligence.

And there is one more layer that I keep coming back to. Once we introduce forecasting — and especially this idea of “model as you go” -something shifts again. SPM is no longer just explaining the past. It is no longer only guiding present decisions. It starts to simulate the future.

What will I earn if I close this deal?

How would my payout change under a different plan?

What happens if I shift focus this quarter?

 At that point, the system becomes something else. Not just a system of interpretation. Not just a system of guidance. But a space where possible outcomes can be explored. Almost like a sandbox. And that raises an even deeper question. If forecasts influence decisions, and decisions influence behaviour…then those modelled futures don’t just stay hypothetical. They start shaping reality.

So now we are not only governing data. We are not only governing intelligence. We are beginning to govern possible futures.

 If we extend the model:

 Inner circle → Reality (what is happening)

Outer circle → Simulation (what could happen)

 AI sits between them. Governance surrounds both.

entry.title: Re: Receive Events from Event Mesh of Integration Suite to CAP Node.js Applications
entry.content:
Hi @vansyckel 

I need to understand if there are a path to migrate old project that use EM with EMIS.

I am required to migrate from standalone Event Mesh to EMIS and use CAP apps as receivers and consumers of messages that also pass through the Integration Suite. We are required to switch to EMIS because, once our contract with SAP BTP expires, we will no longer be able to use EM. How can I perform the migration if SAP CAP is not supported? Are there any best practices or workarounds I can implement?

Thanks a lot

Andrea

I am working in a company dealing with services and they get invoices for the next months services and those are to be paid upfront. In this case, they are posting GR to be able to post Invoice and run the Deferrals program but this is not really working fine if you ant to activate Budget control.

entry.title: SLED mandatory check ignored in Fiori App F1043 (Post Goods Receipt) - Works in MIGO (S/4 Private)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition and facing a discrepancy between GUI (MIGO) and Fiori (F1043) regarding the Shelf Life Expiration Date (SLED) mandatory check.

The Context: Our business requirement is to force users to enter the Expiration Date manually during Goods Receipt (101) without providing a Production Date.

  • Material Master (MARA): MHDHB (Total Shelf Life) = 0 / MHDRZ (Min. Rem. Shelf Life) = 1.

  • Configuration (V_159L): * Plant Check: Active.

    • Movement Type 101: Set to "1" (Enter Expiration Date).

The Issue:

  • In MIGO (GUI): Everything works perfectly. The system identifies the material is SLED-relevant (due to Min. Rem. Shelf Life > 0) and since Total Shelf Life is 0, it forces a manual entry of the Expiration Date.

  • In Fiori App (F1043): The field "Shelf Life Expiration Date" is NOT mandatory. Users can post the GR without any date, which bypasses our inventory controls.

In summary, how can I force the SLED to be mandatory in the Fiori "Post Goods Receipt" app when Total Shelf Life is 0, since it already works correctly in MIGO?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Document Management System (DMS) - JPG/PNG Thumbnails
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I am configuring Web DMS for Product Master Data thumbnails. The DMS customizing in SPRO is working correctly — I can see the generated thumbnail in transaction CV04N. I have maintained the following settings:

  • Cross‑Application Components

  • Document Management

  • General Data

  • Set Up Workstation Application for Thumbnails (Images)

The user can upload a JPG file in DMS under Product Master Data(FIORI), but the thumbnail is still not displayed on the Basic Data screen of the Product Master.

Can you give me some suggestion ? What is missing, and where exactly do I need to configure Web DMS so that thumbnails appear in the Product Master web page?

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
entry.content: Week 4 Completed : Package ZAC_2246894

entry.title: Re: Issue in VL10B – Multiple Outbound Deliveries Created...
entry.content:
Dear @vishnu-1996,

In Public Cloud if VL10B creates two outbound deliveries for two items of the same STO the root cause should be checked in the shipping determination data, not in VL10B itself. In Public Cloud, SAP documents that shipping point determination is controlled by SSCUI 102385, 102126 and 102387 and that storage-location-dependent shipping point determination also applies to replenishment deliveries based on a stock transport order. SAP also confirms that for STOs in Public Cloud the issuing storage location is determined from SD customizing during delivery creation with SSCUI 102130 relevant for the default setup. Therefore, if both STO items must be combined into one outbound delivery they must determine the same shipping point and the same delivery-relevant shipping result during delivery creation. The recommended check is to compare the two items for differences in shipping point, shipping condition, route and issuing storage location determination and then review in CBC / SSCUI 102385 / 102126 / 102387 / 102130

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003546236

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002829420

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to create custom fields to Central invoice Management / Ariba Invoicing
entry.content: Are you on next-gen Ariba Invoicing? Yashvant

entry.title: How to Migrate Current-Year Acquired Assets in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Using Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are operating in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment and are currently migrating assets into a newly created company code.

Using the Migration Cockpit (Migrate Your Data app), along with the migration objects:

  • Fixed Asset – Master Data
  • Fixed Asset – Posting

we have successfully migrated assets acquired prior to the current financial year by maintaining cumulative values (prior-period assets).

However, we are facing challenges when trying to migrate assets acquired during the current financial year. We are unable to identify the correct approach to handle these current-year acquisitions using the available migration objects.

Could you please guide us on the following:

  • What is the recommended approach to migrate current-year asset acquisitions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • Should these be handled via migration objects or through manual/operational postings (e.g., asset acquisition postings)?
  • Are there any best practices or standard procedures to follow for such scenarios?

Any guidance or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Siva Nagi Reddy

entry.title: Candidate Fraud Detection: Reducing Hiring Risks
entry.content:
But what’s “candidate fraud”? The term refers to intentional deception regarding identity, qualifications, employment history, or credentials during the hiring process. This is a serious issue that can compromise the integrity of your recruitment efforts.  

Do you want to know how SAP Fieldglass features can help you to mitigate risks regarding candidate fraud during your selection processes? Then let’s dive into a 5-blog series to see how these features can help you to identify red flags and make more assertive decisions! 

In this first blog, we’ll check some tips and tricks that can help you during the candidate selection process. In the next 4 blogs, we’ll cover the following stages of the hiring process: 

  • Before the interview 
  • Hiring process 
  • Offer and start 
  • Post-hiring  

Candidate Selection 

This is the first step in finding the right candidate. It requires patience, precision, and attention to detail when reviewing the information that candidates provide. In this initial phase, it’s crucial to follow security best practices, such as: 

  • Verifying candidate profiles to confirm authenticity of credentials and work history  
  • Using trusted sources to source candidates   
  • Reviewing applications for duplicates or profiles with inconsistent information  
  • Researching the candidate online and comparing with the information provided (on social media or by reverse image searching, for example) 

Now, let’s check some SAP Fieldglass features that can help you to detect fraudulent information and choose the best candidates. 

Security ID 

A Security ID is a unique code made up of numbers and/or letters. It helps guarantee that each applicant is unique and reduces the risk of duplicate or fraudulent profiles. Also, ensures that only authorized candidates can access and update their profiles, preventing unauthorized users from falsifying their data. 

Recognizing a worker through distinctive security credentials also lets you monitor a worker's background across multiple assignments and service agreements, providing insight into the duration of a worker's tenure. 

You can define a Security ID format as either manually defined or auto-generated. 

Custom Fields and Activity Items for Supplier Evaluation 

You can create Custom Fields and Activity Items for supplier evaluation, enhancing the process by enabling more detailed data collection and automated workflows. Here are some examples of how fields and items you can add to enhance your selection process: 

  • Enhanced Candidate Profiles: You can add custom fields to capture additional, pertinent information about candidates that might not be covered by standard fields. This can include details such as previous employment verification, educational qualifications, certifications, and references. 
  • Compliance Checks: You can add custom fields to track compliance documents such as work permits, licenses, or criminal background checks.  
  • Supplier Performance Monitoring: You can add activity items to track supplier evaluation to guarantee that the suppliers are providing legitimate and qualified candidates. Poorly performing suppliers who frequently submit fraudulent candidates can be identified and potentially removed from the supply chain. 

Questionnaires for Suppliers 

You can create a Questionnaire for suppliers to complete if you need more details from a job seeker. Suppliers may be required to attest through questionnaire responses that they’ve verified the worker's identity, background, and qualifications, making the supplier directly responsible for any fraudulent misrepresentations. 

Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis 

Besides the most well-known features above, SAP Fieldglass also offers Machine Learning features that can be used in the selection process. These features can be used to detect candidate fraud by analyzing resumes, identifying inconsistencies, and enabling predictive security in hiring decisions.  

In the Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis page, you can see all the features that can be used in this stage of the hiring process.   

Now that you’ve learned some features that can help in your candidate selection process, let’s see in our next blog how to explore techniques before the interview, so you get prepared and ready to detect possible red flags.  

 

 

For the purposes of this guide, “candidate fraud” means deliberate misrepresentation of identity, credentials, work history, or qualifications during recruitment. This guide is preventive and educational; it does not make legal determinations.

entry.title: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SAP SuccessFactors Performance and Goals Management
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 1H 2026 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 1H 2026 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1- AI-Assisted Performance Insights Available in All Step Types:

Managers can now gain insights into employees' performance when they're involved in iterative or collaborative steps. Previously, they could only use this feature in single-role steps.

This enhancement benefits managers by providing consistent access to AI-assisted performance insights across all review step types, improving the efficiency and effectiveness of performance evaluations.

      Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

      Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected the Performance Management Feature Settings>Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms option.
  • You've purchased the AI units license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • You've consented to the AI Usage Acknowledgment Statement.
  • You've selected the AI Services Administration>Assisted Performance Insights option.
  • You've selected the Form Template Settings>Enable Assisted Performance Insights for Manager Review option for the intended form template.

2- Business Rule Enhancements on Configuring Dynamic Content for Latest Performance Management Forms:

The Change Field Visibility, Editability, and Required Status on Forms rule scenario has been renamed to Configure Dynamic Form Content, and incorporated with more functionality, such as setting field values, limiting rating field options, changing section visibility, and using the Get Count of Ratings function.

In the If section of the business rule, you can now define conditions using more fields and the Get Count of Ratings rule function.

These enhancements offer greater flexibility for modifying form content based on user input, enabling you to automate a wider range of scenarios in performance reviews.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

Using this rule scenario requires the following configurations. For step-by-step procedures and more information on the new use cases, see Creating a Business Rule to Change Field Visibility, Editability, and Required Status on Forms.

  • You've selected the following options in Performance Management Feature Settings:
    • Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms
    • Enable business rules for Performance Management
  • In the form template, you've properly configured the fields or sections for which you want to create a rule.
  • You've used the Configure Dynamic Form Content rule scenario to create a business rule for the form template.
  • You've assigned the rule to the Form Opening and Form Saving triggers, and to the Field Change trigger as needed.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator Permissions>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings
  • Administrator Permissions>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12
  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Configure Business Rules
  • Administrator Permissions>Assign Rules in Talent>Assign Rules for Performance Management

3- Configure Goal Statuses That Don't Generate Home Page Cards:

In Admin Center>Manage Templates, we introduced a new configuration setting named Do Not Generate Cards on Home Page for Goal Management.

With this setting, you can choose which goal state values will not generate cards on the home page. As critical follow-up actions, you must save the template and choose the Refresh button. This is to make the setting effective and to update existing goals with those statuses so they no longer appear on the home page.

To prevent configuration conflicts, the same state value can't be selected for both Do Not Generate Cards on Home Page and Trigger Completion. If they overlap, you'll be prompted to adjust the selections before saving.

Previously, it wasn't possible to exclude specific statuses from generating cards, meaning statuses like Cancelled or Postponed could still generate cards and distract users.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

Apart from the configuration setting in Manage Templates, you can also set no-generate-card="true" to one or more state values in the goal plan template to complete the configuration.

4- Default Expanded or Collapsed State for Section Descriptions on Latest Performance Management Forms:

With the latest experience of Performance Management forms, you can now set section descriptions to be expanded or collapsed by default. The section description area has also been improved for better accessibility.

In the Form Template Settings admin tool, there's a new option called Expand Form Section Descriptions on First Opening. By default, this setting is disabled, meaning section descriptions are collapsed when users open a form for the first time. If enabled, section descriptions will be expanded by default on first opening. Once a user manually expands or collapses a description, their preference is saved in their browser and remains the same when they reopen the form.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms in the Performance Management Feature Settings admin tool.
  • If you want section descriptions to be expanded by default, make sure you've selected Expand Form Section Descriptions on First Opening in the Form Template Settings admin tool.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates permission.

5- Enhanced Mass Routing Functionality in Performance and 360 Form Inbox:

From the Inbox folder on the Performance>My Forms page, users can now use the Send to Next Step and Send to Previous Step buttons to route up to 5,000 forms at once, a significant increase from the previous limit of 20. Routing results are shown in a dialog or sent by email, depending on the situation.

Enablement: Automatically on

6- Evaluate Next Role Skills and Competencies in Latest Performance Management Forms:

With the latest Performance Management form experience, you can now configure a new type of section that allows users to evaluate the skills or competencies required for an employee's next job role in their career path. You can also use business rules to control the visibility of this section, displaying it only when specific conditions are met.

In the Manage Templates admin tool, after you add an Attribute section (either a skill or competency section) and select Job Specific, you will see a new dropdown field with two options: Current Job Role and Next Job Role. If you choose Next Job Role, the skills or competencies associated with an employee's next role will be auto populated into this section when the form is created.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • Career Path is enabled in Provisioning.
  • You've created career paths that include next job roles.
  • You've enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms.
  • You've configured a job-specific skill or competency section for the next job role in the form template.
  • If you want to display the next-role section based on conditions, make sure you've configured a business rule for this section using the Configure Dynamic Form Content rule scenario.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • For next-role skills or competencies to appear on the form, the user who creates the form must have the User Permissions>Miscellaneous Permissions>Career Path>View permission for the employee's career path that contains the next role.
  • As in other skill and competency sections, users need permission to view items for the next role:
    • If you have migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub, users need the User Permissions>Growth Portfolio>View permission for Attribute, Attribute Type, Growth Portfolio, Proficiency Level Scale, and Tag.
    • If you have not migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub, and the Competency object is set to secured in the Configure Object Definitions admin tool, users need the User Permissions>Miscellaneous Permissions>Competency permission.

7- Hide Extra Overall Score Displays on Latest Performance Management Forms:

With the latest experience of Performance Management forms, you can now choose to show the overall score only as a clickable value in the form header, hiding it from the Overall Score popover and the end of the form. This helps avoid repetitive information when your form has only one summary section.

By default, when the Overall Score feature is available, users can view the score in three places:

  • As the clickable Overall Score value in the form header
  • At the end of the Overall Score popover
  • At the end of the form

Now, you can hide the score from the last two places by enabling the setting Hide Additional Overall Score Information in the Form Template Settings admin tool.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms.
  • To use this feature, make sure you've selected Hide Additional Overall Score Information in the Form Template Settings admin tool.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates permission.

8- Latest Experience of Performance Management Forms Automatically On by Default:

The new Performance Management form experience is now automatically enabled in all systems, except those that use the legacy Goal Management or Job Description Manager. If needed, administrators can switch back to the legacy experience.

Enablement: Automatically on

9- Latest Experience of Team Overview in Performance Management:

The latest Team Overview provides a cleaner, more accessible user interface with a template-based view and enhanced navigation, while retaining functionalities of the legacy experience.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

You've selected Enable the latest experience of Team Overview option in the Performance Management Feature Settings admin tool.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

To access Team Overview, users must have the User Permissions>Performance>Team Overview Access permission.

10- Latest Experience of Team Overview in Performance Management:

You can now allow users to manually rate competencies, in addition to rating behaviors, in the latest experience of Performance Management forms.

Previously, when you enabled Rating on behavior for a competency section in the Manage Templates admin tool, users could only rate the behaviors mapped to each competency. The competency ratings were automatically calculated based on the behavior ratings and couldn't be changed by users.

Now, when you enable Rating on behavior, you can further enable Use manual competency rating instead of behavior rating calculation (only applicable to the latest form experience). When enabled, users can manually rate both competencies and their mapped behaviors. Competency ratings must be entered manually and are no longer calculated from behavior ratings. Behavior ratings are for reference only.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms.
  • You've enabled Rating on behavior in the Manage Templates admin tool.
  • To use the new feature, make sure you've enabled Use manual competency rating instead of behavior rating calculation (only applicable to the latest form experience) in the Manage Templates admin tool. This setting is disabled by default.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12 permissions.

11- Latest Experience of Team Overview in Performance Management:

A new admin tool, Mass Change Goal Plan States, is now available for you to change performance goal plan states for multiple employees at once, without needing to open each employee's goal plan individually.

The new tool provides a centralized way to change multiple employees' goal plans to a new state in bulk. It supports uploading a CSV file with up to 30,000 state changes and includes system validation to ensure only valid changes are applied. To handle large volumes efficiently, the system processes the changes in a background job so your other work remains unaffected. You can check the job's progress with the job Mass Change Goal Plan States in Scheduled Job Manager. After the job is completed, you'll receive an email summary so you can easily review the results of the mass change.

Access to this tool is managed through a new role-based permission named Mass Change Goal Plan States.

Enablement: Automatically on

Configuration Requirements:

The latest Goal Management is enabled.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator Permissions>Manage Goals>Mass Change Goal Plan States permission.

12- New Rule Scenario: Add Custom Validations to Form Fields:

When the latest experience of Performance Management forms is enabled, you can configure business rules that display warning or error messages on forms when certain fields meet the conditions you defined.

Here are some examples of applying this rule scenario:

  • If the manager gives the highest score for more than 75% of an employee's goals, there will be a warning message suggesting reassessment. The manager can choose to adjust their ratings or ignore the warning.
  • If the second-level manager rates a skill lower than the direct manager, a warning message will be displayed, advising them to match or exceed the direct manager's rating.
  • If the HR manager gives an overall potential rating below 4 while selecting the "Ready for Promotion" checkbox, there will be an error message that highlights this discrepancy, requiring revision before submission.

The warning and error messages are created in Manage Data and then associated with specific rule conditions in Configure Business Rules. When forms are saved or being sent to the next step, and the conditions are satisfied, users can see relevant messages by opening the validation dialog from the form footer. All messages generated by rules are grouped under the categories Pay attention to {n} issues found in the form content. and Solve {n} issues found in the form content., as shown in the following screenshot:

This new rule scenario allows you to apply additional validation logic to the form, ensuring a more flexible and thorough review process that meets your organization's requirements.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected the following options in Performance Management Feature Settings:
    • Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms
    • Enable business rules for Performance Management
  • You've created a Message Definition object that contains the message to be displayed.
  • You've used the Add Custom Validations to Form Fields rule scenario to create a business rule for the intended form template.
  • You've assigned the rule to the Form Saving trigger.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Manage Data
  • Administrator Permissions>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings
  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Configure Business Rules
  • Administrator Permissions>Assign Rules in Talent>Assign Rules for Performance Management

13- New Rule Scenario: Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome:

When the latest experience of Performance Management forms is enabled, you can configure business rules to automatically launch follow-up forms, such as performance improvement plans, when initial forms are submitted with certain ratings. The rules can also inform users about the conditional form launches and carry some information from the initial forms into the follow-up forms.

The new rule scenario, Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome, involves two types of forms: the initial form and the follow-up form. When the initial form is routed to the next step and its ratings meet the criteria defined in your rule, a follow-up form is automatically created for the same employee. This process is recorded in the audit trail of the follow-up form, with the following system comment: Automatically launched when {username} completes the "{stepName}" step of the "{formTitle}" form (ID: {formId}).

Here are some examples of applying this rule scenario:

  • If the potential rating is "High", launch a leadership development form.
  • If the overall competency rating is "Below Expectations", launch an individual development plan form.
  • If the overall performance rating is "Below Expectations", launch a performance improvement plan form.

In addition to configuring an automatic launch, you can let users know about it by displaying a custom message in the confirmation dialog for sending the form to the next step. You can see an example message in the following screenshot, which reads: "If you send this form with a potential rating of 'High', a leadership development form will be created for this employee." The message is created in Manage Data and then configured in the rule.

You can also use the Get Assessment Summary rule function to specify which information from the initial form will appear on the follow-up form. This provides reviewers with relevant details from the original assessment to support the follow-up process. The new form can display the following data in a predefined text area field: individual goal, skill, and competency ratings; overall ratings; and custom fields in the custom section.

The following screenshot shows a custom section on a follow-up form, with a text area field named "Annual Assessment Results". The field content comes from the initial form, including two competency ratings, an overall performance rating, and content from a custom text area.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected the following options in Performance Management Feature Settings:
    • Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms
    • Enable business rules for Performance Management
  • You've configured form templates for the initial form and the follow-up form.
  • (Optional) If you want to carry information from the initial form into the follow-up form, make sure the follow-up form template is configured with a text area custom field in a custom section to display the data. Also make sure that all roles are granted Read permission for this field so the data won't be edited.
  • (Optional) If you want to display a message about the conditional launch in the confirmation dialog for form routing, make sure you've created a Message Definition object that contains the message.
  • You've used the Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome rule scenario to create a business rule.
  • You've assigned the rule to the Form Routing to Next Step trigger.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator Permissions>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings
  • Administrator Permissions>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12
  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Configure Business Rules
  • Administrator Permissions>Assign Rules in Talent>Assign Rules for Performance Management

14- Template-Level Permission Control for the Route Form Tool:

As a Role-Based Permissions (RBP) administrator, you can now control which forms are accessible in the Route Form admin tool by applying the Route Document permission to specific form templates. This enhancement is available in Performance Management, 360 Reviews, and Compensation.

In the Manage Permission Roles admin tool, under Administrator Permissions>Manage Documents>Route Document, you can now use a setting to specify which form templates this permission applies to. If this permission is already granted to a role, the setting defaults to All, so the role can continue to view and route forms from all templates using Route Form. If you restrict this permission to specific templates, the role can only access these templates and forms created from them.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

If you've already added Route Document permission to any permission roles, please note that it will be temporarily removed after the Preview or Production release on Friday. It will be automatically restored over the weekend. If you need this permission during the interim period, you can manually add it back so related tasks can be performed.

15- Writing Assistant and Legal Scan Available in Latest Performance Management Forms:

Writing Assistant and Legal Scan, two existing features in the legacy experience of Performance Management forms, are now available in the latest experience as well.

With proper configurations, users can now access Writing Assistant and Legal Scan on the latest form.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms in the Performance Management Feature Settings admin tool.
  • To use Writing Assistant, make sure:
    • Writing Assistant is selected in Provisioning>Company Settings.
    • Enable Writing Assistant is selected in the Form Template Settings admin tool.
    • Writing Assistant is selected in the Custom Elements dialog in the Manage Templates admin tool, if you want to enable this feature for competency custom fields.
  • To use Legal Scan, make sure:
    • Legal Scan is selected in Provisioning>Company Settings.
    • Enable Legal Scan is selected in the Form Template Settings admin tool.
    • Disable Legal Scan on Route is not selected in the Form Template Settings admin tool, if you want Legal Scan to run automatically when forms proceed along the steps.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • You have the Administrator>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings permission.
  • You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12 permissions.

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: Re: SAP JCO : Limiting output of RFC SAPTUNE_GET_SUMMARY_...
entry.content: Great to know that. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP BI 4.3: What’s New - Automation Framework renamed as Workflow Assistant
entry.content: As per our discussion with SAP, workflow assistant seems to be having issues so they are no longer providing that option to change data source directly. There is no automated option as of now as per my knowledge.

entry.title: Re: What Is Agentic AI and Why SAP Teams Are Paying Attention in 2026
entry.content:
Thanks @Fabio-Martins @luis_almada @Denver_Fletcher91 and good to hear from you all and your insights really motivates me to write more blogs! Great question @Denver_Fletcher91 and you’re right to frame it from a risk and governance perspective.

Most agentic AI systems today are still LLM‑based at their core, but they are not “just an LLM” acting freely. Architecturally, they are better understood as LLMs embedded within a control framework rather than standalone decision-makers.

A typical agentic setup looks something like this:

  • LLM as the reasoning component (interpret intent, plan steps, generate options)
  • Deterministic orchestration layer (rules, policies, workflows, state machines)
  • Tool/action interfaces with explicit constraints (APIs, transactions, approvals)
  • Observability and guardrails (logging, validation, human-in-the-loop where needed)

So yes—the reasoning engine is probabilistic. That implies there will never be mathematical “perfection,” even with clean data and well-defined processes. Agentic systems acknowledge this explicitly rather than pretending otherwise.

Where risk is managed is not by eliminating ambiguity, but by bounding where ambiguity is allowed to exist:

  • The agent can propose actions, but execution is constrained by policy and authorization.
  • The action space is finite and pre-approved, even if the choice among them isn’t deterministic.
  • Confidence thresholds, schema validation, and runtime checks reduce unpredictable outputs.
  • For high-impact decisions, escalation paths (human review, dual control) are mandated.

So the governance question shifts from:

“Can the AI ever make an unexpected decision?”

to:

“What decisions is the AI allowed to make autonomously, under what conditions, and with what auditability?”

In enterprise contexts (including SAP use cases), agentic AI is deliberately designed to separate cognition from control. The LLM reasons; the system governs.

Your point about “windows of opportunity” is absolutely valid—that’s exactly why agentic architectures emphasize policy-driven orchestration, traceability, and reversible actions rather than unconstrained autonomy.

In short:

  • ✅ Yes, LLMs are foundational
  • ❌ No, they are not deployed as unconstrained actors
  • ✅ Governance is achieved through architectural controls, not blind trust in the model

That statement you described isn’t a flaw—it’s the reason agentic AI is being engineered as a system, not a model.

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: WEEK 3 COMPLETED : ZAC_2289764

entry.title: Re: Optional Task: Use Joule CDS Unit Test Generation
entry.content:
Done.

Package : ZAC_1620808

entry.title: Re: Video series on flexible programming model for SAP Fiori elements
entry.content:
The link to the videos it´s not working , there´s a message in Youtube informing that the associated account has been cancelled.

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP TS-Based Supply Finite Heuristic - TRATIO
entry.content:
Hi thank you very much!

However, https://me.sap.com/notes/0003225730 also mentions the following: "Finite heuristic does not consider quotas defined via the CUSTOMERRATIO, LOCATIONRATIO, or PRODUCTIONRATIO key figures, as well as ratios defined as an attribute of the source of supply, instead it uses the values from the Quota key figures (MINCUSTOMERQUOTA, MAXCUSTOMERQUOTA, MINLOCATIONQUOTA, MAXLOCATIONQUOTA, MINPRODUCTIONQUOTA, MAXPRODUCTIONQUOTA, MINRECEIPTQUOTA, and MAXRECEIPTQUOTA)" ...

But is it not true that with the 'Check mode' the operator can not yield a solution if there is a production ratio or the customer ratio greater than 1? @carsten_schumm
